# Pro-Gro 550 LED Grow Journal Harvest Pics (RETIRED)
* * *
`Hello, please note that the Pro-Gro 550 light has been **retired**`
[`**Learn about current LED grow lights**`](https://www.growweedeasy.com/5-best-led-grow-lights-2025-cannabis-yields-speed-bud-quality)
* * *
This is an abridged version of this grow journal.
[Click here](https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-grow-light-review-2013) to view the **entire** LED grow journal with…
  * Notes from grower Endive during each part of the LED grow
  * View ALL the pictures of these plants from the vegetative stage to harvest
  * Get answers to the most frequently asked questions about LEDs

Just click right here:  
<https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-grow-light-review-2013>
Introduction by Nebula Haze
Many of you have been writing in with questions about growing marijuana with LED grow lights.
As you may know, GrowWeedEasy.com covered [LED grow lights](https://www.growweedeasy.com/LED-grow-lights-marijuana) about a year ago with Endive, today’s guest writer.
Endive has graciously allowed us to republish one of his most recent grow journals with the Pro-Grow 550 LED light. You can see **all** of Endive’s LED grow journals on his website [growmedical420.com](http://growmedical420.com/).
And without further ado, enjoy….
Pro Grow 550 LED Grow Light Journal Highlights  
by [Endive](https://web.archive.org/web/20201025075555/http://www.growmedical420.com/about/)
**Grow Space:**  
Enclosed 47″ x 53″ area
**Strains:**
1 – Critical x Sensi Star (4-gallon fabric pot)
1 – Landrace Afghani (3-gallon fabric pot)
**Growing Medium:**
[Soil – Fox Farm Ocean Forest](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B08W2CB7GV?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B08W2CB7GV&pd_rd_w=xrheN&pf_rd_p=887084a2-5c34-4113-a4f8-b7947847c308&pd_rd_wg=vfT1a&pf_rd_r=YHGP768E6T1NXY13XBBF&pd_rd_r=4c0e533f-e8fb-443e-9c44-18e011ca23ea&smid=A83LZ4XZH4503&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUEyNEdTNDlSU1lKWlpBJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwNjQ0ODI3MzFMRTEyQTYyTU5JTyZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwNjg1NzUxWkRROFNVRDRQRUJaJndpZGdldE5hbWU9c3BfZGV0YWlsJmFjdGlvbj1jbGlja1JlZGlyZWN0JmRvTm90TG9nQ2xpY2s9dHJ1ZQ%3D%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e19d67b4b9001a7d0b1d78d437bfea2e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Nutrients:**
General Organics “GO Box” Line (discontinued)
**Lighting:**
1 x  _Pro-Grow 550 LED Grow Light_ **(retired)**
Power Consumption: Actual draw is 470w (in full illumination mode)
Light Schedule: 18/6 veg & 12/12 flower
**[](http://www.hydroponicshutaffiliate.com/idevaffiliate.php?id=100&url=13)**
Click here to see pictures of the entire grow (from vegetative stage to harvest) along with notes from grower Endive, and answers to FAQs about LED grow lights:  
<https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-grow-light-review-2013>
Critical+ Sensi Star – At Harvest
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/sprawling-web-sticky-Critical-Sensi-Star-colas-and-buds.jpg)  
Sticky Critical Sensi Star colas & buds, just before harvest
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Critical-Sensi-Star-tops-grown-under-Pro-Grow-550-LED-grow-light.jpg)  
Critical Sensi Star tops, grown under the Pro-Grow 550 LED grow light
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/lower-side-colas-got-enough-light-fatten-up.jpg)  
Lower side colas got enough light to fatten up
The ample lighting from the Pro-Grow 550 penetrated well enough to catch the lower, outer cola tips. Inner buds that did not get the direct lighting were thankfully few, but very small.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/damn-fine-bud-under-hydroponicshut-LED-grow-light.jpg)  
Not the wrist thick colas like the [X5-300 LED](http://www.hydroponicshutaffiliate.com/idevaffiliate.php?id=100&url=14) produced, but damn fine!
I left the small inner buds on the branches till the last cola was removed & manicured.
From there the small buds were trimmed and thrown into the hash box to dry.
Critical+ Sensi Star Weigh-In (After Being Dried)
After the buds were cleaned up, I weighed the buds and placed them into curing jars.
At the weighing, I was the pleasantly surprised with the total – 167 grams of trimmed bud total, or 5.8oz!
Critical+ Sensi Star Final Weight: 
**167 grams, or 5.8 ounces**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Critical-Sensi-buds-are-stored-in-jars-and-get-daily-burpings-until-they-are-properly-cured.jpg)  
While stored in jars, these Critical Sensi buds will get daily “burpings” until they’re properly cured
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Critical-Sensi-Star-buds-grown-under-Pro-Grow-550-LED-grow-light.jpg)  
Critical+ Sensi Star buds, grown under a Pro-Grow 550 LED grow light
The Afghan Kush – At Harvest
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Afghan-Kush-just-prior-to-harvest.jpg)  
The Afghan Kush just prior to harvest
The colas closer to the light were noticeably thicker with rounded tops as opposed to lower ones which formed narrow “spear-tipped” colas & buds.
The lower buds and colas are still beautiful, but the tops are far more impressive as far as weight/yield than I first thought they would be.
Examples of the lower colas:
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/lower-limbs-cut-early-from-Afghan-Kush-ready-to-trim-and-dry.jpg)  
Lower limbs cut early from the Afghan Kush, ready to trim & dry
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Afghan-Kush-lower-narrow-colas-are-rock-hard-density-with-trichomes-for-miles.jpg)  
Afghan Kush’s lower colas were narrow, yet still rock hard with trichomes for miles!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Pro-Grow-550-LED-produced-fine-Afghan-Kush-colas.jpg)  
The Pro-Grow 550 LED produced some very fine Afghan Kush colas
Afghan Kush Weigh-In (After Being Dried)
After taking her sweet, dank time drying, the Afghan Kush is now weighed and in jars for curing.
The final weights were taken prior to the buds going into curing jars. I was very curious to see how the Afghan would stack up against the Critical Sensi Star that finished at 167 grams of prime, manicured buds. 
Visually the Afghan looked to have smaller colas & buds in contrast to the Critical Sensi Star, but the density of the inner bud and trichome coating make for heavy, rock solid buds.
From looking at her, I figured on the Afghan hitting just over the 4 ounce mark, maybe… I was very pleased with the final weight!
Afghan Kush Final Weight:  
**156 grams, or 5.5 ounces**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Afghan-Kush-buds-grown-under-Pro-Grow-550-LED-grow-light.jpg)  
Our Afghan Kush buds grown under a Pro-Grow 550 LED grow light
There isn’t much aroma until you break a bud open, the taste is very neutral, slightly oily. But the best part is, they are all covered in trichomes like below:
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/covered-in-trichomes-ranging-from-clear-to-amber-its-easy-to-see-why-Afghan-Kush-is-used-to-make-hash.jpg)  
Covered in trichomes… it’s easy to see why Afghan Kush is used to make hash
So to recap the Pro-Grow 550 LED grow…
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Afghan-Left-Critical-Sensi-Right-Pro-Grow-550-LED-grow-light.jpg)  
Afghan (L), Critical Sensi (R), Pro-Grow 550 LED grow light (center)
The PG-550 uses 470 watts actual draw and I’d put it up against my 600 watt HPS any day.
If you take into account the “75 watts per square foot” rule for how much light you need for your marijuana grow, it dictates you’d need 1500 watts of HID to cover the same area as the area I covered with a single PG-550 LED grow light.
**Time From Seed to Final Harvest:** 4.5 months
**Critical Sensi Star:** 167 grams
**Afghan Kush:** 156 grams
**Total Yield:** 323 grams (11.4 ounces)
That’s roughly .68 grams per watt, in soil, without supplements, no [CO2](https://www.growweedeasy.com/co2-marijuana-yields), and at 23 watts per sq ft.! This light made hardly a blip on my electricity bill!
The more we grow the more we learn 😉
**About The Author: Endive**
I am a legal, medical cannabis patient and grower in the state of Oregon.
Long story short… After many trial and errors with pain treatment for a degenerative cervical condition, I came to trying cannabis as a therapy for control of pain and nerve/muscle spasms at the suggestion of a relative. After much research, I was truly amazed at the medical and healing properties of marijuana.
**Free Yourself – Get Educated!**
Don’t give in to the notion that pharmaceuticals are safer. And if you find medical marijuana is right for you, remember that you need not be held ransom to pay dealers and dispensaries for quality meds. Honestly, if I can do this, anyone can.
View more grow journals on my website: [http://growmedical420.com](http://growmedical420.com/)
* * *
Final Thoughts by Nebula:
LEDs are getting better and better every year. A few short years ago, they were a brand new technology in grow lights, and it’s continuing to look like they are set to become the grow lights of the future.
If you’re willing to invest in a quality brand LED light like a Pro Grow, you’re buying from a company who specifically serves the medical marijuana community, and actually tests their lights on marijuana plants.
As you can see from the results Endive is getting (and this is just one of his grows, he has dozens of grow journals on his website [growmedical420.com](http://growmedical420.com/)) the Pro Grow LED grow lights are giving many HPS growers a run for their money.
At this point in the game, I still recommend getting an [MH/HPS grow light combo](https://www.growweedeasy.com/hps-grow-lights-setup) if the initial price tag of quality LED grow lights is too much for you. MH/HPS grow lights will give you consistently great yields and cost considerably less than LEDs. I don’t recommend trying to save money with cheaper LEDs as many cheap LED grow lights out there just aren’t built to work for a high-yield flowering plant like marijuana.
If initial cost is not a concern, and you want to experiment with the power of LEDs (which will give you potent buds with a unique aroma) or if heat and electricity usage are big concerns, than you may want to learn more about [modern LED grow lights](https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-upgrade-guide) for growing cannabis.
